models.generation_context: 1st-gen CT4, introduced 2020; final model year — production ends June 25, 2026, order books closing week of April 20, 2026.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's compact rear- or all-wheel-drive luxury sedan, slotting below the CT5. Standard turbocharged 2.0L I4 (237 hp) with an available 2.7L Dual-Volute turbo I4 (310 hp). 2026 is the CT4's final model year in North America.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a CT4-specific 2025 VDS score (study measures 3-year-old cars). Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power's 2026 U.S. Vehicle Dependability Study (released Feb 2026), well below the 204 PP100 industry average; Consumer Reports 2026 ranked Cadillac 18th with brand score 41/100. CT4 owner reports cite occasional electrical/infotainment glitches but no chronic powertrain issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: No CT4-specific APEAL score published for 2025/2026. Owner reviews skew positive on styling and handling, mixed on cabin refinement vs. European rivals.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the CT4's sharp handling, responsive steering, and value pricing relative to BMW 3 Series and Audi A4. Common critiques focus on a tight rear seat, dated infotainment hardware, and an interior that falls short of European peers on material quality. The 2.7L Dual-Volute upgrade adds real punch but is locked behind higher trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the CT4's styling, smooth ride, and Cadillac comfort features; complaints center on tight rear-seat legroom, limited USB ports, and occasional infotainment quirks.

trims.notes: MSRP shown is RWD pre-destination. AWD adds approximately $2,000. NHTSA has not yet rated 2026 CT4; IIHS has not awarded TSP/TSP+ to current CT4. Adaptive cruise, BSM, RCTA are optional/package items on Luxury — set to false per spec convention. Cadillac.com consumer pages returned maintenance error during research so image URLs are page placeholders pending follow-up scrape.

notable_other: Optional 2.7L Dual-Volute Turbo I4 (310 hp, 350 lb-ft) with 10-speed automatic

changes: Adds optional 2.7L Dual-Volute Turbo I4 (310 hp / 350 lb-ft) availability with 10-speed automatic
Adds standard adaptive cruise control, blind spot monitoring with rear cross-traffic alert, rear automatic braking
Adds ventilated front seats and heated steering wheel
Adds wireless phone charging and panoramic sunroof
Upgrades to Bose 14-speaker audio (with available AKG)
Upgrades to 18" alloy wheels (vs 17")
Adds Super Cruise availability

trims.notes: MSRP shown is RWD with standard 2.0L. 2.7L upgrade adds approximately $4,500. AWD adds approximately $2,000.

notable_other: Sport pedals and unique interior trim

changes: Adds sport-tuned suspension and Sport drive mode
Adds gloss-black exterior accents, unique grille, and gloss-black 18" wheels
Adds sport pedals and unique interior trim
Adds standard adaptive cruise, BSM, RCTA, rear automatic braking
Adds ventilated front seats, heated steering, panoramic sunroof, wireless charging
Upgrades to Bose 14-speaker audio (with available AKG)
Adds optional 2.7L Dual-Volute Turbo I4 (310 hp) availability

trims.notes: MSRP shown is RWD pre-destination. AWD adds approximately $2,000. 2.7L upgrade available.

models.notes: Final model year — production at GM Lansing Grand River ends June 25, 2026. Order books for new CT4 sedans close week of April 20, 2026. Cadillac.com consumer pages returned 'Site Maintenance' error during research; specs reconciled across cars.com, KBB, Edmunds, gmauthority press coverage, and EPA fueleconomy.gov. CT4-V and CT4-V Blackwing split as separate models per spec §6.4 performance-variant rule. Adaptive cruise, BSM, and RCTA on the Luxury trim require a Driver Assist Package per Cadillac marketing — recorded as false on Luxury and true on Premium Luxury/Sport where they appear to be standard.

models.generation_context: CT4-V V-Series performance sedan, introduced 2020; 2026 is final model year.
models.model_summary: V-Series performance variant of the CT4 with the standard 2.7L Dual-Volute Turbo I4 retuned to 325 hp / 380 lb-ft, paired with a 10-speed automatic and rear- or all-wheel drive. Adds Brembo brakes, magnetic ride control, performance suspension and unique V-Series styling. 2026 is its final model year.

reliability.summary: No CT4-V-specific JD Power VDS score for 2025. Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS; CR 2026 brand score 41/100 (rank 18). Low volume limits Consumer Reports sampling.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July). No CT4-V-specific APEAL data.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the CT4-V as a competent sport sedan that splits the difference between the standard CT4 and the wilder Blackwing. The 325-hp 2.7L offers strong mid-range torque and the chassis grips well, but feel and feedback fall short of segment benchmarks. AWD availability and Brembo brakes add value to a model in its final year.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 owner-review sample. Owners of recent-MY CT4-V cite strong straight-line performance, comfortable daily-driver behavior, and good Bose audio; gripes echo the standard CT4's tight rear seat and dated infotainment hardware.

trims.notes: Sole-trim model line per Cadillac marketing — CT4-V has its own cadillac.com page distinct from CT4. AWD adds $500 (CT4-V AWD: $50,600 RWD pricing reported as $52,395 post-DFC by gmauthority — base before DFC is $50,600). NHTSA has not rated 2026 CT4-V; IIHS has not awarded TSP. EPA verified ID 49245 maps to 2026 CT4 V 2.7L Turbo 23 MPG combined. Driver display assumed 12.0" based on prior-MY brochure pending cadillac.com verification.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — own cadillac.com page (cadillac.com/sedans/ct4-v) distinct from CT4. Final model year. Sole-trim atomic rule applied: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null on the single V-Series trim.

models.generation_context: CT4-V Blackwing, introduced 2022; 2026 is final model year. Last manual-transmission Cadillac in production.
models.model_summary: The CT4-V Blackwing is Cadillac's highest-performance compact sedan: a 472-hp twin-turbocharged 3.6L V6 paired with a standard six-speed manual or an optional 10-speed automatic. RWD only, with carbon-ceramic brake option and aggressive V-Series chassis tuning. 2026 is the final model year; order books closed in April 2026.

reliability.summary: No CT4-V Blackwing-specific JD Power VDS due to low volume. Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100; CR 2026 brand score 41/100 (rank 18).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July). Low-volume model unlikely to receive individual APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Critics regard the CT4-V Blackwing as one of the finest enthusiast sedans of its era — a true rear-drive, manual-available sport sedan with linear twin-turbo V6 power, exceptional steering feel, and Magnetic Ride Control. Its discontinuation is widely lamented. Common gripes are the tight rear seat and dated infotainment, both inherited from the CT4.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 owner sample. Recent-MY owners praise driving engagement, steering feel, and the manual gearbox; complaints are minimal beyond the small interior package.

notable_other: Performance Traction Management with 5 drive modes

trims.notes: Sole-trim model line. 10-speed automatic optional ($3,175). Carbon-fiber 1 / 2 / 3 packages available. EPA ID 49249 = manual 18 MPG combined; ID 49248 = automatic 19 MPG combined. Adaptive cruise is optional on Blackwing per Cadillac marketing — set to false. NHTSA/IIHS not rated. Final model year — production ends June 25, 2026.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — own cadillac.com page. Sole-trim atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null. Order books closed week of April 20, 2026 per Cadillac announcement.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen CT5, introduced 2020; major mid-cycle refresh for 2025 added 33" curved display and Google Built-in. Final model year — production ends late 2026.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's midsize rear- or all-wheel-drive luxury sedan, slotting above the CT4. Standard 2.0L turbo I4 (237 hp) with available 3.0L Twin-Turbo V6 (335 hp) on Premium Luxury. 2026 is the CT5's final model year in North America.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a CT5-specific 2025 VDS score. Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS. The CT5 made Consumer Reports' Best Luxury Cars to Buy 2026 list, signaling steady reliability among GM's near-luxury sedans.

customer_satisfaction.summary: No CT5-specific APEAL score published for 2025/2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the refreshed CT5's new 33-inch curved display with Google Built-in, sharper styling, and engaging RWD handling. The optional 3.0L Twin-Turbo V6 transforms acceleration; the base 2.0L is adequate but unremarkable. Critics still cite an interior that doesn't quite match BMW/Audi peers on perceived quality.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the new 33" display, ride comfort, and styling; common complaints focus on rear visibility and lack of hybrid powertrain availability.

notable_other: 33" Horizon display with Google Built-in (Maps, Assistant, Play Store)

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination (post-DFC $50,095). AWD adds approximately $2,000. 3.0L Twin-Turbo V6 upgrade available on Premium Luxury only (335 hp). NHTSA has not yet rated 2026 CT5; IIHS has not awarded TSP. EPA ID 49284 verified.

changes: Adds sport-tuned suspension and unique chassis tuning
Adds gloss-black exterior accents and unique Sport grille
Adds Sport bucket seats with unique stitching
Upgrades to 19" gloss-black wheels (vs 18")
Adds black mesh interior accents and unique trim
3.0L Twin-Turbo V6 not available (Premium Luxury only)

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination (post-DFC $51,995). AWD adds approximately $2,000. Sport trim is 2.0L Turbo only — V6 not offered on Sport for 2026.

models.notes: Final model year. CT5-V and CT5-V Blackwing split as separate models per spec §6.4. Major mid-cycle refresh for 2025 added 33" Horizon display and Google Built-in carries through 2026. CT5 has only two non-V trims for 2026 (Premium Luxury and Sport); the plain 'Luxury' trim was discontinued. Cadillac.com consumer pages returned 'Site Maintenance' error during research; specs reconciled from KBB, Edmunds, gmauthority, and EPA.

models.generation_context: CT5-V V-Series sedan, introduced 2020; final model year for 2026.
models.model_summary: V-Series performance variant of the CT5 with a standard 3.0L Twin-Turbo V6 producing 360 hp and 405 lb-ft, paired with a 10-speed automatic and RWD or AWD. Adds Brembo brakes, Magnetic Ride Control 4.0, and V-Series-specific styling. 2026 is its final model year.

reliability.summary: No CT5-V-specific JD Power VDS published. Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS; CR 2026 brand score 41/100 (rank 18).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July). No CT5-V-specific APEAL data.

professional_reviews.summary: The CT5-V is positioned as a daily-driver V-Series — quicker than the standard CT5 thanks to the 360-hp Twin-Turbo V6, but more refined than the wild Blackwing. Reviewers praise the smooth power, communicative steering, and updated 33-inch curved display, while noting that AWD optional Blackwings remain the enthusiast's choice.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 sample. Owners praise the V6 sound, smooth shifts, and Magnetic Ride. Few complaints beyond fuel economy and lack of a manual transmission option (manual is reserved for the Blackwing).

notable_other: 33" Horizon curved display with Google Built-in
Sport bucket seats with V-Series stitching

trims.notes: Sole-trim performance variant. AWD optional ($2,500). EPA ID 49279 RWD 21 combined; ID 49280 AWD 20 combined; ID 49281 AWD V also 20 combined. NHTSA/IIHS not rated.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — own cadillac.com page. Sole-trim atomic rule applied: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null.

models.generation_context: CT5-V Blackwing, introduced 2022; 2026 is final model year. Last 6.2L supercharged V8 manual-transmission Cadillac in production. Bespoke F1 Collector Series (26 units) launching mid-2026.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's range-topping performance sedan: a 668-hp supercharged 6.2L V8 with standard six-speed manual (10-speed automatic optional). RWD only, with Magnetic Ride Control 4.0, Brembo brakes, and 200+ mph top speed. Won The Drive's Best Car of 2026. 2026 is the final model year.

reliability.summary: No Blackwing-specific reliability data. Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS; CR 2026 brand score 41/100 (rank 18). Hand-built LT4 V8 has a strong durability track record.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July). Low-volume model unlikely to receive individual APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Critics universally praise the CT5-V Blackwing as an enthusiast's farewell — a 668-hp supercharged V8 sedan with a standard manual transmission, sublime steering feel, and a chassis tuned at the Nurburgring. Won The Drive's Best Car of 2026. The 2026 refresh adds the 33-inch curved display and Google Built-in. Common gripes: thirsty engine, expensive options.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners cite engaging manual gearbox, monstrous power, and surprisingly comfortable daily-driver behavior. Few complaints beyond fuel economy.

notable_other: Standard 6-speed manual with Active Rev Match (10-speed auto optional)
Custom Launch Control with Line Lock
Deep Ocean Appearance Edition (200 units) and F1 Collector Series (26 units) special-edition variants

trims.notes: Sole-trim model. Adaptive cruise is optional/package on Blackwing (set false). EPA ID 49283 = manual 15 combined; ID 49282 = automatic 15 combined. NHTSA/IIHS not rated. Final model year; Curated by Cadillac one-of-one program and F1 Collector Series (26 units, mid-2026 production) available.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — own cadillac.com page. Sole-trim atomic rule. The Drive's Best Car of 2026 per GM press release Feb 2026.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen XT5, introduced 2017. Final model year of current generation — GM confirmed production continues to end of 2026 before a redesigned 2nd-gen replaces it for the US.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's mid-size 5-passenger luxury crossover SUV. Available in Luxury, Premium Luxury, and Sport trims with 2.0L Turbo I4 (235 hp) or 3.6L V6 (310 hp; standard on Sport). Final year of current generation.

reliability.summary: XT5 was a JD Power 2025 VDS top-three Midsize Premium SUV finalist. Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (released Feb 2026), well below the 204 industry average. CR 2026 ranked Cadillac 18th overall with brand score 41/100; per-model CR predicted reliability for XT5 not separately published.

professional_reviews.summary: The XT5 is regarded as a comfortable, easy-to-live-with mid-size luxury SUV that prioritizes ride quality over driving sport. Reviewers note its quiet cabin, intuitive controls, and standard tech, while criticizing the smaller 8-inch infotainment (vs newer Cadillac EVs), lack of hybrid availability, and aging architecture entering its final year.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the ride comfort, build quality, and roomy back seat. Common complaints center on outdated infotainment hardware and modest cargo space behind the rear seats.

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($48,595 post-DFC). AWD adds approximately $2,000. EPA ID 50001 verified 24 combined.

notable_other: Optional 3.6L V6 (310 hp / 271 lb-ft) with AWD
Wood or carbon fiber interior trim

Adds standard adaptive cruise, BSM, RCTA, rear automatic braking, driver attention monitoring
Adds ventilated front seats, heated steering, memory seats
Adds UltraView panoramic sunroof, head-up display, wireless charging
Upgrades to Bose Performance Series 14-speaker audio
Upgrades to 20" diamond-cut alloy wheels (vs 18")

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($55,295 post-DFC). AWD adds approximately $2,100.

notable_other: Black exterior trim and unique grille

Sport-tuned adaptive suspension and Brembo brakes

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($64,595 post-DFC). AWD and V6 standard. EPA ID 50005 verified 21 combined for AWD V6.

models.notes: Final model year of current generation. 2nd-gen XT5 will replace this for the US after 2026. Cadillac.com consumer pages returned maintenance error during research; specs reconciled from KBB, Edmunds, gmauthority, and EPA. ADAS standard-equipment lines may vary by package — Luxury trim Driver Assist features set per known Cadillac trim-walk.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen LYRIQ, introduced 2023 on GM Ultium platform. 2026 updates include NACS port migration and refreshed trim structure.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's first all-electric SUV on GM's Ultium platform. Single-motor RWD (365 hp, 326 mi range) or dual-motor AWD (515 hp, 319 mi range). 102-kWh battery, 190-kW DC fast charging. Trims split into Luxury (chrome) and Sport (gloss black) styling lines across base, Premium, and Signature equipment levels.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a LYRIQ-specific VDS score (vehicle is too new for the 3-year study). Cadillac brand placed #2 among premium brands in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100 (industry avg 204), but Consumer Reports flagged the LYRIQ as a weak point in its Dec 2025 reliability report, citing software issues common to early Ultium EVs.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the LYRIQ's striking exterior design, hushed cabin, and the massive 33-inch curved display with crisp Cadillac graphics. Available Super Cruise is among the best hands-free systems on the road. Critiques focus on early-software glitches, missing IIHS TSP (due to a Poor headlights rating), and a competitive EV segment that's grown rapidly since launch.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the styling, quiet cabin, and Super Cruise. Common complaints: occasional software/infotainment bugs, slower-than-advertised peak DC charging in cold weather, and complicated charge-curve management.

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($60,695 post-DFC). EPA ID 49631 verified 92 MPGe combined, 326 mi range. IIHS rated 2025 LYRIQ with Good crashworthiness but Poor headlights — no TSP/TSP+ award. 2026 trims split into Luxury and Sport styling lines; this 'Luxury' is the base of the Luxury family.

Sport styling line is its own trim_family per spec §7 (different cosmetic treatment from Luxury). Treated as separate base trim of the Sport family per spec §6.2 sole-trim/singleton-family rule. MSRP shown pre-destination ($61,195 post-DFC). Premium Luxury / Premium Sport / Signature Luxury / Signature Sport are higher equipment tiers within the Luxury and Sport families — treated as packages per spec §6.5 to keep trim count manageable and not split per multiple commercial-equipment levels.

models.notes: Trim structure per Cadillac: Luxury and Sport styling families × base/Premium/Signature equipment tiers (6 nominal trims). Per spec §6.5 the equipment tiers are treated as packages within the two cosmetic trim families (Luxury and Sport), keeping the catalog manageable. Two sole-trim families recorded per sole-trim atomic rule. LYRIQ-V (615 hp performance variant) split as separate model per §6.4.

models.generation_context: First-ever electric V-Series Cadillac; introduced for the 2026 model year. Production at GM Spring Hill, TN.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's first-ever electric V-Series vehicle. Dual-motor AWD producing 615 hp / 650 lb-ft with Velocity Max launch mode for 3.3-second 0-60. 102-kWh battery yields 285 mi EPA range. Available in V-Series and V-Series Premium trims.

reliability.summary: Too new for JD Power VDS. Shares Ultium platform with LYRIQ — software/charging caveats from CR's Dec 2025 report likely apply.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers call the LYRIQ-V the quickest Cadillac ever and praise its locomotive shove, the AKG sound system, and Super Cruise. The chassis is more grand-tourer than canyon-carver, and high curb weight blunts agility versus rivals. Range drops to 285 mi vs the standard LYRIQ's 326, but charging behavior is unchanged.

owner_reviews.summary: Few owner reviews — sales began Spring 2025. Early owners praise straight-line speed and quiet cabin.

trims.notes: Sole-trim model line; only AWD. MSRP shown pre-destination ($78,595 post-DFC per news.gm.com release). EPA ID 49633 verified 80 MPGe combined, 285 mi range. V-Series Premium adds Nappa leather and starts at $83,795 post-DFC — treated as a higher-equipment package per spec §6.5 rather than a separate trim to maintain catalog atomicity.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — own cadillac.com page at /electric/lyriq/v-series. Sole-trim atomic rule applied. V-Series Premium tier folded into notable_other rather than split as separate trim per §6.5 package rule.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen OPTIQ, introduced 2025 as Cadillac's compact electric SUV. Built on GM Ultium platform.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's entry-point all-electric compact SUV. Single-motor RWD (315 hp, 317 mi range) or dual-motor AWD (440 hp, 303 mi range). 85-kWh battery. Trims split into Luxury and Sport styling families with base and Premium equipment tiers.

reliability.summary: Too new for JD Power VDS sample. Cadillac brand #2 among premium in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100. Shares software platform with LYRIQ, where CR flagged reliability concerns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consider the OPTIQ one of the better-value EVs in its segment thanks to standard Super Cruise availability, the 33-inch curved display, and the upscale AKG audio. The interior packaging is tighter than LYRIQ but more usable than expected. Critics note an aggressive charging-curve drop-off and a stiffer ride versus rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Early-MY ownership skews positive on styling, tech, and value. Some report software glitches similar to early LYRIQ owners.

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($50,895 post-DFC). EPA ID 49949 verified 105 MPGe combined, 317 mi range. AWD adds approximately $2,000 (440 hp / 498 lb-ft / 303 mi range).

notable_other: Unique Sport grille and 20" gloss-black alloys

trims.notes: Sport styling line as separate trim_family per §7 (different cosmetic treatment). MSRP shown pre-destination ($51,395 post-DFC). Premium Luxury and Premium Sport equipment tiers folded as packages per §6.5.

models.notes: Two sole-trim families (Luxury and Sport) per Cadillac's commercial structure. Premium tiers folded as packages per §6.5. OPTIQ-V split as separate model per §6.4.

models.generation_context: First-ever performance variant of the OPTIQ, introduced for 2026 model year. Production launches Spring 2026; limited availability.
models.model_summary: Performance V-Series variant of the OPTIQ. Dual-motor AWD producing 519 hp / 650 lb-ft with Velocity Max for 3.5-second 0-60. 85-kWh battery yields 275 mi EPA range. Adds Brembo brakes, adaptive dampers, AKG 19-speaker audio, and unique V-Series styling. Magnus Launch Edition available 2026.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: OPTIQ-V is new for 2026 and too recent for a model-specific JD Power VDS or CR predicted reliability score. Brand-level: Cadillac ranked #2 among premium in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100 (industry avg 204). Consumer Reports 2026 ranked Cadillac 18th with brand score 41/100; CR's December 2025 reliability report flagged Cadillac EVs (LYRIQ, OPTIQ) for software/infotainment issues common to early Ultium platform vehicles.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); model is also brand-new for 2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the OPTIQ-V as a surprisingly quick small luxury EV — 0-60 in 3.5 seconds — with a chassis tuned more for daily enjoyment than track work. Adjustable bolster front seats and adaptive dampers add appeal. Range drops 42 mi vs the base OPTIQ.

trims.notes: Sole-trim model line. MSRP shown pre-destination ($67,300 post-DFC). EPA ID 50026 (11 kW charger, 15 modes) = 92 MPGe combined; ID 50027 (19 kW charger) = 83 MPGe. Curb weight per Cadillac press 'more than 5,400 lb'.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — own cadillac.com page at /electric/optiq/v-series. Sole-trim atomic rule applied. Limited availability launching Spring 2026; Magnus Launch Edition (2026 only) folded as package per §6.5.

models.generation_context: All-new for 2026 — Cadillac's first 3-row all-electric SUV. Slots between LYRIQ and Escalade IQ.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's new three-row all-electric SUV. Single 102-kWh battery with dual-motor AWD across the lineup producing 615 hp / 650 lb-ft. EPA-estimated 305 mi range. Trims: Luxury, Sport, Premium Luxury, and Platinum. 0-60 in 3.7 seconds.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: VISTIQ is all-new for 2026 and too recent for model-specific JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted reliability. Brand-level: Cadillac ranked #2 among premium in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100 (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked Cadillac 18th overall with brand score 41/100; CR's December 2025 reliability report flagged the Ultium platform (shared with LYRIQ) for software/infotainment issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); model is also brand-new for 2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the VISTIQ's combination of three-row capability with V-Series-level performance (615 hp standard across all trims), the 23-speaker AKG Studio audio with Dolby Atmos, and an interior that feels appropriately upscale for the price. Concerns center on 305-mi range vs heavier rivals and unproven Ultium software stability.

notable_other: Air suspension with Magnetic Ride Control 4.0
Up to 79 mi of range added in 10 min DC fast charge

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($77,395 post-DFC). EPA ID 49636 verified 86 MPGe combined, 305 mi range. CCS1 plug confirmed via Cadillac press materials (NACS upgrade pending for future MY).

notable_other: Gloss-black exterior trim and unique grille

Sport-tuned air suspension calibration
Body-color wheel arch moldings

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($79,590 post-DFC). All-AWD, 615 hp standard across VISTIQ lineup.

models.notes: All-new model for 2026. Single battery and powertrain across all trims (102 kWh, 615 hp dual-motor AWD). Trims differentiated by trim_family for image sharing per spec §7.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Escalade IQ, introduced for 2025 MY. Built on GM Ultium platform with 24-module 205-kWh battery.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's all-electric full-size flagship SUV. 205-kWh battery, dual-motor AWD producing up to 750 hp / 785 lb-ft in Velocity Max mode. Up to 460 mi EPA-estimated range. Trims: Luxury, Sport, Premium Luxury, Premium Sport.

reliability.summary: Too new for JD Power VDS sample (introduced 2024 as 2025 MY). Shares Ultium platform with LYRIQ where CR flagged early-software issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Escalade IQ as a technological tour-de-force — 460-mi range, 800V architecture, and a wall-to-wall 55-inch display. The 9,000-lb curb weight makes it a luxury cruiser more than an athlete, and pricing pushes well past the gas Escalade. Standard Super Cruise and rear-wheel steering shrink the parking footprint.

owner_reviews.summary: Early-owner sentiment positive on range, presence, and Super Cruise. Common gripes: heavy curb weight reduces real-world range, tight charging infrastructure for ultra-large EVs.

notable_other: Magnetic Ride Control 4.0 with air suspension
Up to 100 mi range added in 10 min DC charge

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($129,795 post-DFC). EPA has not yet published a specific 2026 Escalade IQ entry on PowerSearch as of research date — fuel_economy source set to brand-make-page per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. MPGe and range figures from manufacturer cadillac.com/electric/escalade-iq/specs.

models.generation_context: Extended-length variant of Escalade IQ, all-new for 2026 model year. Adds 4.2 inches of length, primarily benefiting 3rd-row legroom.
models.model_summary: Extended-length Escalade IQ — 4.2" longer for more 3rd-row legroom (36.7" vs 32.3") and headroom. Same 205-kWh battery and dual-motor AWD producing up to 750 hp / 786 lb-ft in Velocity Max. Up to 460 mi EPA-estimated range. Same four trims as Escalade IQ.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Escalade IQL is all-new for 2026 — too recent for model-specific JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted reliability. Brand-level: Cadillac ranked #2 among premium in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100 (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked Cadillac 18th overall with brand score 41/100; shares Ultium platform with LYRIQ where CR flagged software concerns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); model is also brand-new for 2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the IQL as the rare three-row EV that genuinely accommodates adult passengers in the 3rd row, thanks to the 4.2" added length. Edmunds testing showed 500+ mile real-world range. Critiques mirror the IQ: heavy curb weight, large turning radius despite rear-wheel steering.

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($132,795 post-DFC). EPA 2026 IQL not yet on PowerSearch; source set to brand-make page per spec §4 fallback. MPGe estimated based on Escalade IQ ratio scaled for IQL added weight.

EPA 2026 IQL entry pending — fuel_economy sources cite brand-make page per spec §4 fallback. MPGe scaled down 2 from IQ based on Cadillac press range delta.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Escalade, introduced 2021; mid-cycle refresh for 2025 added 55" display and refreshed exterior. 2026 trim renaming: base now '1SA', former 'Premium Luxury' renamed 'Luxury'; former 'Premium Luxury Platinum' renamed 'Platinum Luxury'; 'Sport Platinum' renamed 'Platinum Sport'.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's flagship full-size body-on-frame luxury SUV. Standard 6.2L V8 (420 hp) with optional 3.0L Duramax Turbo-Diesel I6 (277 hp / 460 lb-ft) on most trims. Magnetic Ride, air suspension, 55-inch Total OLED Display, Super Cruise available.

reliability.summary: Cadillac brand ranked #2 among premium brands at 175 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS; CR 2026 brand score 41/100 (rank 18). Escalade-specific data not separately published. Long production history with 6.2L V8 supports moderate reliability confidence.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Escalade's monumental presence, sumptuous Platinum interiors, hushed cabin, and the 55-inch curved Total OLED Display. Critiques focus on body-on-frame ride compromises versus competitors, fuel economy (17 combined for V8 2WD), and price creep into six figures.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise presence, towing capability, interior space, and Super Cruise. Common gripes: fuel economy, infotainment learning curve, real-world cargo behind 3rd row tight.

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($91,100 post-DFC) — formerly known as the base 'Luxury' trim before 2026 renaming to RPO code '1SA'. EPA ID 50138 verified 17 combined for 2WD V8. 4WD adds $3,000. Adaptive cruise is optional on 1SA — set false.

models.notes: Escalade ESV split as separate model per spec §6.4 — own commercial line and separate model page. Escalade-V split as separate model per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule. EPA IDs verified: 50138 (2WD V8 17 combined), 49899 (4WD V8 16 combined). Diesel option available across Luxury through Platinum Sport trims (~$24-28 combined per EPA prior MY).

models.generation_context: Extended-length Escalade (15" longer than standard). 5th-gen, 2025 mid-cycle refresh carries through 2026.
models.model_summary: Extended-wheelbase variant of the Escalade — 226.9" overall length (vs 211.9" standard). Adds 1.8" of 3rd-row legroom and ~21.7 cu ft more max cargo. Same 6.2L V8 / Duramax diesel powertrains as Escalade. Same five trims (1SA, Luxury, Sport, Platinum Luxury, Platinum Sport).

reliability.summary: Cadillac brand #2 among premium in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100. Escalade ESV shares all mechanicals with Escalade.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers say the ESV is the Escalade for those who genuinely use the third row and need cargo room behind it. Same strengths and weaknesses as standard Escalade with added length yielding meaningful interior space gains.

owner_reviews.summary: Same as Escalade. Owners cite the extra cargo behind 3rd row as the key differentiator.

notable_other: Extended cargo behind 3rd row (~16 cu ft more than std Escalade)

trims.notes: MSRP shown pre-destination ($94,100 post-DFC). ESV combined MPG 1 lower than Escalade due to longer body. EPA does not separately list 2026 ESV — fuel economy figures are estimated from Escalade EPA ID 50138 with 1-MPG combined adjustment per historical ESV pattern.

models.notes: Escalade ESV markets on cadillac.com/suvs/escalade as a length variant but is treated as separate model per spec §6.4 due to commercial separation (own MSRPs, own pricing per gmauthority breakdown). Images shared with standard Escalade family (cadillac.com/suvs/escalade single configurator). EPA 2026 ESV not separately listed — fuel economy estimated.

models.generation_context: V-Series performance variant of Escalade; introduced 2023. Standard-length and ESV body styles available.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's highest-performance full-size SUV: a supercharged 6.2L V8 producing 682 hp / 653 lb-ft, paired with a 10-speed automatic and standard AWD. Magnetic Ride Control, air suspension, Brembo 6-piston front brakes, quad exhaust. 0-60 in 4.4 seconds.

reliability.summary: No Escalade-V-specific reliability data; shares LT4 V8 with CT5-V Blackwing and Corvette Z06.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers are amazed at the absurdity of a 682-hp supercharged 3-row family SUV that hits 60 mph in 4.4 seconds while still seating 7 in luxury. Tradeoff: 13 combined MPG and a premium of nearly $80k over a Platinum Luxury Escalade.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited sample. Owners praise raw power, exhaust note, and surprising ride quality. Fuel cost is the universal complaint.

features.sound_system: AKG Studio Reference 36-speaker with Dolby Atmos
notable_other: Magnetic Ride Control with Adaptive Air Ride
ESV body style available (Escalade-V ESV at $171,000)

trims.notes: Sole-trim model line. MSRP shown pre-destination ($170,595 post-DFC). EPA ID 49898 verified 13 combined for AWD supercharged V8. ESV body style folded as note (separate $171,000 post-DFC) per spec §6.5 — Escalade-V ESV uses same powertrain and equipment, body length only changes.

models.notes: Performance variant split per spec §6.4 — has own cadillac.com/suvs/escalade/v-series page. Sole-trim atomic rule applied. ESV body style available at $171,000 — treated as length option per spec §6.5 to keep performance V-Series as single-model entry; image set could be expanded with ESV variant later.

models.generation_context: Hand-built bespoke ultra-luxury EV sedan; production began 2024. Each unit one-of-one customer-commissioned at GM Global Technical Center in Warren, Michigan.
models.model_summary: Cadillac's hand-built flagship ultra-luxury EV sedan, customer-commissioned via Cadillac House at the GM Global Technical Center in Warren, Michigan. 111-kWh battery, dual-motor AWD producing 655 hp / 646 lb-ft. EPA-estimated 303 mi range. Smart-glass roof, four pillar-to-pillar displays, near-infinite material customization. Production limited to small annual volume.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: not sampled - low volume hand-built model
reliability.summary: CELESTIQ is too low-volume for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports sampling (each unit hand-built, one-of-one). Brand-level: Cadillac ranked #2 among premium in JD Power 2026 VDS at 175 PP100; CR 2026 brand score 41/100 (rank 18).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); CELESTIQ also too low-volume for typical APEAL sampling.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers (Edmunds, TopGear) describe the CELESTIQ as a remarkable showcase of bespoke American luxury — every panel hand-finished, with the smart glass roof, AKG audio, and four-screen pillar-to-pillar dash setting a new Cadillac high-water mark. Critiques are about value vs Rolls-Royce/Bentley pedigree and the modest 303-mi range for the price.

owner_reviews.summary: No public owner reviews — extremely low volume.

features.sound_system: AKG Studio Reference 41-speaker with Dolby Atmos
notable_other: 55" pillar-to-pillar 4K LED display + 8" front passenger comfort display
Smart Glass roof with 4 controllable zones (premium pattern now standard)
Hand-built at GM Global Technical Center

trims.notes: Sole-trim model. MSRP $400,000 is starting point — every CELESTIQ is bespoke and final out-the-door pricing can exceed $500k depending on commissioned options. No public destination fee (delivery handled as part of bespoke commission). EPA ID 50197 verified 81 MPGe combined, 303 mi range. Dimensions sourced from Wikipedia compilation of Cadillac press materials as cadillac.com consumer pages returned maintenance error during research; cross-checked against Edmunds first-drive (2025-08).

models.notes: Sole-trim model. Hand-built bespoke EV sedan — orderable via cadillac.com/electric/celestiq/declaration-form and the Cadillac House at Vanderbilt facility. 2026 changes: starting price increased to ~$400k (from ~$340k), 8-year connected-services subscription now standard, smart-glass premium pattern now standard. Cadillac.com/electric/celestiq page returned maintenance error during research; specs reconciled from gmauthority pricing announcement, EPA fueleconomy.gov, US News, Edmunds first-drive review, and Wikipedia compilation of Cadillac press materials.
